what_it_does: This file is a generated surface specification for view A6 in area A; it records the UI addresses the surface covers, its journey membership, the components it builds to, and the design-system sources and lint gate it depends on.
observations: Generated artifact from a Claude Code session, with a note that addresses are never invented.
Surface id is A6, area A, title 'Force-rerun + cache legibility', status planned, desktop-only.
Represents engineering concerns ENG-force and ENG-memo.
Home journey is J1 and journeys[] contains only J1; cross_journeys[] is empty.
Lists nine ui:// addresses spanning board, canvas, chat, inspector, and run scopes.
build_to names components board, canvas, chat, inspector, run; a design_lint command; and a tokens-to-CSS mapping.
standouts: Addresses include force, memo-gate, freshness, eng-memo, and act/force variants.
Build_to treats design_lint as a 'FORM gate' and tokens as 'the only look-source'.
conventions: Follows the surface-spec schema: id, area, title, platforms, represents, status, home_journey, journeys, cross_journeys, addresses, build_to.
Uses directory path as a journey spine (tree).
References concrete UI addresses rather than defining new ones.
Explicitly declares empty cross_journeys and symbols arrays.
concerns: Provenance note ('Built by a Claude Code session') and process rule ('never invent an address') are embedded in the generated artifact and may stale.
Status is planned with no owner, date, or acceptance criteria.
design_lint targets canvas/app/src while the surface is desktop-only; scope alignment is unclear.
Tokens path uses a Unicode arrow, which is cosmetic and may complicate tooling.
notes: A6 is a planned desktop surface spec for J1 that wires force-rerun and memo-gate UI addresses into board/canvas/chat/inspector/run and pins build outputs to a single token source and a lint gate.
questions: What concrete behavior does 'Force-rerun + cache legibility' describe?
How do the nine addresses map onto the five listed components?
What is the B3 web referenced for journeys/cross_journeys?
When and by whom is the design_lint gate run?
Why is this surface desktop-only when some components (chat, canvas) may exist on other platforms?
purpose_vs_actual: Matches: the file states it is a generated surface spec carrying address-refs, and it does exactly that by listing addresses, journeys, and build targets for view A6.
apparent_themes: Single source of truth for visual tokens
intention_signals: It appears to define a planned desktop surface that exposes force-rerun controls and cache/freshness memo-gates across multiple UI components, while enforcing that visual design comes from one token file and code passes a lint gate.
novelty: It explicitly treats a design-lint command as a 'FORM gate' and a token-to-CSS pipeline as 'the only look-source', making build quality and visual consistency first-class surface dependencies.
contribution: Provides a concrete, address-level surface definition for A6 that a fused system can use to know which UI addresses, components, journeys, and build outputs belong to this view, plus explicit build constraints (lint gate, token source).
summary_for_embedding: company/design/blueprint/surfaces/J1/A6.json is a planned desktop surface specification for view A6 in journey J1, listing nine UI addresses across board, canvas, chat, inspector, and run components, representing ENG-force and ENG-memo, and declaring build targets including a design-lint gate and a single token-to-CSS source.
intention_for_embedding: It was generated to record which UI addresses and build outputs define the A6 desktop surface for force-rerun and cache-legibility features without inventing addresses.
